candidate: Daria Wrubel — Berkeley City Council D1
org_research: The Pro-Housing Democratic Caucus of California is a caucus within the California Democratic Party that endorses candidates who support building more housing at all income levels. Their questionnaire focuses exclusively on housing policy — market-rate development, height limits, missing middle, ADUs, historic districts, and key state legislation. A definitive yes or no answer on SB 79 (Wiener, 2025) is required for endorsement.

questions.question: What seat are you running for? I.e. what is the name of the office?
Tell us about your lived experience with housing. Do you own / rent?
Does housing sold without public subsidy (i.e. market-rate housing) benefit the general public? Does it provide affordability overall?
Do you believe in / would you support a plan to build housing in historic districts, where new homes are currently not allowed?
Do you support the separate sale of backyard homes (also known as ADUs), pursuant to AB 1033? Would you work to support / sponsor legislation that facilitates separate sale?
How supportive are you of single stairwell buildings?
How important are height limits to you? What do you see as the benefits and drawbacks of a height limit?
Would you be supportive of a 20-story building in your City?

Tell us about how you react to allowing more missing middle housing across neighborhoods where there are typically just detached single-family homes. What is your favorite type of missing middle housing?
Do we have a housing crisis?

Would you have supported SB-79 (Wiener, 2025)? Or, did you vote on this legislation / cast any votes on whether to support or oppose this legislation? A definitive answer (yes or no) to one of these questions is required to obtain the PDC Endorsement.
